item.title: Topolski's Chronicle, V.1 No.3, 1953
item.creator: Topolski, Feliks, 1907-1989
item.description: Topolski's Chronicle is a series of broadsheets created by Feliks Topolski and distributed bimonthly to subscribers from 1953 through 1982. Topolski was a superlative draughtsman and covered current events with an artistic, unique style.

pages.typeset_text:
TOPOLSKI's CHRONICLE No. 3 Vol. 1 1953
SOHO FAIR
sketch_descriptions: A broad landscape sketch showing an industrial scene with cranes, scaffolding, and a tower on the left, a river or road running through the middle, seagulls flocking in the sky, and a large dome-shaped building to the right, resembling St. Paul's Cathedral.

A crowded interior scene with loosely drawn figures, some with detailed facial profiles, including a seated man in the foreground; assorted heads and faces are clustered together in a lively, almost chaotic arrangement, suggesting the bustle of a fair or public gathering.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle captures scenes and impressions from the Soho Fair in London, 1953, including a sketch of an industrial riverside landscape and a busy urban crowd, likely representing fairgoers or local residents.

pages.handwritten_text:
South London
Marble Arch
20
Barclay Lager
Troc. Laces[?]
19
sketch_descriptions: A busy street scene in South London with several women in dresses seen from behind, crowds, traffic lights, billboard ads including one for Barclay Lager, and distant city architecture.
A crowded urban gathering possibly near Marble Arch, with figures in hats and coats, including a newspaper seller and clusters of people in motion.
An interior view of a London Underground carriage with passengers seated and standing, a sign reading 'Troc. Laces[?]', and interactions among riders.
A group of women, some seated and some standing, engaged in conversation inside what appears to be a waiting area or compartment.
pages.page_summary: This page captures expressive sketches of everyday city life in South London and Marble Arch, including lively street scenes, crowds, and moments inside public transport.

pages.typeset_text:
"...Enormous, enormous... Paris is mediocre compared with these squares, these crossings, these spacious streets... there is a bustling crowd, a surging traffic... which our busiest and most frequented boulevard cannot parallel. Everything is on a large scale here... actions and time are turned to the utmost possible account; the human being produces and enjoys twice as much as among us..."

NOTES ON ENGLAND by Hippolyte Adolphe Taine (London 1872)

"TOPOLSKI'S CHRONICLE" IS PUBLISHED 24 TIMES A YEAR AT 2s. 6d. A COPY.
YEARLY SUBSCRIPTION RATES (WITH FOLDER AND INDEX) TO ANY PART OF THE WORLD ARE £3.3s. ( $9 or equivalent); SHORTER PERIODS PRO RATA. AIR MAIL BY QUOTATION.
Printed in Great Britain by C. Bednarczyk of Tunbridge Wells from blocks made by the Dome Engraving Co., Ltd and published from 14 Hanover Terrace, London N.W.1, Tel. AMBassador 6059.
Copyright reserved.
sketch_descriptions: Loose expressive sketch of a crowded urban street scene featuring two men in hats conversing in front of a sign reading '8 Mason', a woman standing with arms folded, and parts of a double-decker bus with a sign labeled '88 Victoria', including a seated figure inside.
pages.page_summary: This page from Topolski's Chronicle captures a vibrant street scene in Piccadilly, London, highlighted by a busy intersection with buses and pedestrians. It is anchored by a historical reflection on London’s energetic pace compared to Paris, quoted from Hippolyte Taine.
